title: allocators: Apply manual ASan poisoning to `PoolResource`
summary: This PR adds manual AddressSanitizer (ASan) poisoning to the PoolResource memory allocator to help detect use-after-free bugs in memory sub-chunks that ASan normally can't track.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves Bitcoin Core's development and testing infrastructure by making memory safety bugs easier to detect during development, which may help prevent potential crashes or security vulnerabilities related to memory corruption from reaching production.

title: test: Add `msgtype` to `msg_generic` slots
summary: This PR fixes a Python test framework issue by adding the missing 'msgtype' field to the '__slots__' declaration of the 'msg_generic' class.
potential_rationale: This appears to be a small bugfix that likely enables proper testing of peer-to-peer message handling scenarios, which may help ensure Bitcoin's network protocol implementation is thoroughly validated through automated tests.

title: doc: clarify and cleanup macOS fuzzing notes
summary: This PR cleans up and consolidates documentation about fuzzing on macOS, while noting that macOS fuzzing support is not actively maintained.
potential_rationale: This change likely helps clarify the current state of fuzzing support across different platforms, which may help developers understand testing limitations and potentially guide them toward better-supported fuzzing environments for finding Bitcoin Core bugs.
